description: slack/publish_manifest MCP tool. 8KB publish cap. Replace semantics via pins.list/remove/add. 1/hour rate limit per channel. allowFrom publish gate. Optional agentCard shape matching A2A /.well-known/agent-card.json. Issue #31, ships v0.6.0 conditional. Blocked by 31-A.

description: Tool accepts a manifest object, validates it via the same Zod schema as 31-A, and publishes it as a pinned message in the given channel. Only one manifest per bot per channel.

description: Publish manifest A. Publish manifest B. Assert: at no point are both A and B pinned simultaneously. Assert final state: only B pinned.

description: Document publish flow, 8KB cap, 1/h rate limit, allowFrom gate, replace semantics. Warn about publishing secrets (manifests are public to channel members).

description: Optional agentCard shape matching /.well-known/agent-card.json (0qk.6).

description: Round-trip equivalence, rate-limit, allowFrom gating, replace atomicity (0qk.7-10).

description: Publisher-side protocol section + A2A alignment notes (0qk.11-12).
title: Publisher-side notes and A2A alignment

description: Enforce 8KB on the serialized manifest at publish time. Stricter than 31-A's 40KB read cap (Postel's Law — strict on output, tolerant on input).
title: 31-B.2: Publish-size cap — 8KB (stricter than read)

description: Find any existing pinned manifest from this bot_id. pins.remove it. Post new manifest message. pins.add. Atomic-ish (no two manifests pinned at once, worst case transient gap).
title: 31-B.3: Replace semantics — pins.list → remove prior → post → pins.add

description: Map<channelId, lastPublishAt>. Reject second publish within 1h with clear error. Prevents pinning spam. Not persisted — restart resets.
title: 31-B.4: In-memory rate limiter — 1 publish per channel per hour

description: publish_manifest verifies the caller's user_id is in the top-level allowFrom set. Cross-references existing access control. Non-allowFrom caller rejected with clear error.
title: 31-B.5: Gate — only allowFrom callers may publish

description: Add optional agentCard:{schemas,endpoints,…} field to manifest schema. Shape follows Google A2A spec for future HTTP-level compat. Does not affect Slack pin flow.
title: 31-B.6: Optional agentCard field matching A2A /.well-known/agent-card.json

description: Full cycle. Publish via 31-B, read via 31-A, assert deep-equal (modulo publishedAt timestamp). Uses a real Slack mock / recorded response fixture.
title: 31-B.7: Test — round-trip publish → re-read returns equivalent manifest

description: Publish at t=0 succeeds. Publish at t=30min throws. Publish at t=61min succeeds. Advance fake clock to test.
title: 31-B.8: Test — rate limiter rejects second publish within 1h

description: Call publish_manifest as a user_id not in allowFrom. Assert rejection, assert no Slack API calls made, assert clear error message that does not leak allowFrom contents.
title: 31-B.9: Test — non-allowFrom caller rejected

description: JournalEvent type. SHA-256 hash chain (Schneier-Kelsey 1999). Redaction module for token patterns. Truncation. --audit-log-file CLI flag + SLACK_AUDIT_LOG env. Atomic append (O_APPEND). Zero Slack surface. Issue #30, ships v0.4.1 ahead of v0.5.0. Blocks Epic 30-B.
title: Write every action to a tamper-evident local log

description: TypeScript type + Zod schema. All audit events conform. kind is a tagged enum (tool_call_start, tool_call_end, policy_decision, session_activate, session_deactivate).
title: 30-A.1: JournalEvent type — {ts, kind, toolName, input, outcome, correlationId, prevHash, hash}

description: Write 100 events, flip 1 byte in entry 42's serialized payload, run verifier — must report chain break at 42. Also flip 1 byte in stored hash field — must report break.
title: 30-A.10: Test — tamper detection via chain verification

description: Explain Schneier-Kelsey chain. Show example 3-line log with prevHash chain. Include copy-paste verify one-liner (node -e '...' or a tiny verify script).
title: 30-A.11: Docs — 000-docs/audit-journal-architecture.md hash-chain section + verify command

description: JournalEvent type (5pi.1), SHA-256 hash-chained writer (5pi.2), trusted-anchor timestamp (5pi.3) — the append-only log structure.
title: Append-only log with a SHA-256 chain

description: Token-pattern redaction (5pi.4) and input truncation (5pi.5). Prevents credential leakage before hash + append.
title: Scrub tokens and huge payloads before writing

description: --audit-log-file + SLACK_AUDIT_LOG wiring (5pi.6) and O_APPEND+fsync-on-rotation writer (5pi.7).
title: CLI flag and atomic append to disk

description: End-to-end chain verification over 1000-event log (5pi.8), redaction coverage (5pi.9), tamper-detection via chain verify (5pi.10).
title: Prove the chain catches any tampering

description: Audit-journal architecture doc updates — hash-chain section and verify command reference (5pi.11).
title: Architecture notes and the verify command

description: Schneier-Kelsey 1999. Each line carries prevHash + its own hash. Boot: if log exists, read last line's hash; else prevHash = genesis constant. Single writer to prevent interleave.
title: 30-A.2: SHA-256 hash-chained writer — hash = sha256(prevHash || serialized(event))

description: ts = { wall: ISO-8601 UTC string, seq: monotonic uint64 }. Counter breaks ties inside same millisecond. Prevents adversarial re-ordering within a single clock tick.
title: 30-A.3: Trusted-anchor timestamp (ISO-8601 UTC + monotonic counter)

description: Pre-write transform. Replace matches with [REDACTED:type]. Types: anthropic, slack, github, aws. Pure function. Feeds 30-A writer AND 30-B full mode.
title: 30-A.4: Redaction module — regex matchers for sk-*, xoxb-*, ghp_*, AKIA*

description: Large inputs get collapsed. Pattern: first 200 chars + ' … (N chars omitted) … ' + last 200. Preserves correlation with tool error messages while bounding log size.
title: 30-A.5: Input truncation — first 200 + omitted + last 200 (>500 chars)

description: Opt-in. If unset, no journal writes. If set, validate path writable, open append-only handle at startup. Env var for deployment, flag for local runs.
title: 30-A.6: --audit-log-file CLI flag + SLACK_AUDIT_LOG env var

description: Use fs.createWriteStream with {flags:'a'}. fsync once per N events or on rotation. Rotation: size-based (e.g., 100MB → .log.1). Crash-safe.
title: 30-A.7: Atomic append (O_APPEND on POSIX) + fsync on rotation boundary

description: Generate 1000 events, write them, read back, recompute hash chain, assert identical to stored hashes. Proves writer's chain is correct.
title: 30-A.8: Test — hash chain verifies end-to-end over synthetic 1000-event log

description: Table test: each known token pattern goes in, [REDACTED:type] comes out. Negative cases: random hex does not get redacted. Include edge cases (tokens inside JSON strings).
title: 30-A.9: Test — redaction catches all documented token patterns

description:
lib.ts:284-286 silently falls back to path.resolve() when realpathSync throws on a configured SENDABLE_ROOTS entry. An attacker who creates a symlink at the expected path post-startup could make a previously inaccessible root suddenly accessible with a structurally different (non-canonicalized) check.

Fix: on server.ts bootstrap, call realpathSync on every SENDABLE_ROOTS entry. If any throws, log the failure and exit(1) — don't degrade to lexical resolve(). Failing-closed matches the rest of the security posture.

Acceptance:
- server.ts exits non-zero on missing/inaccessible SENDABLE_ROOTS entry with clear stderr message
- Existing tests still pass (hermetic tests that stub the filesystem continue to work)
- Documented in ACCESS.md / SECURITY.md that SENDABLE_ROOTS must exist at boot
title: infra: Fail-fast at startup when SLACK_SENDABLE_ROOTS entry missing or inaccessible

description:
Landed 29-A.1 without tests. Add server.test.ts coverage for:

- MatchSpec zero-field refinement rejection (all five fields undefined)
- MatchSpec.argEquals empty object rejected (refinement treats as zero fields)
- ttlMs 24h ceiling enforcement (accepts 24h, rejects 24h+1ms)
- ttlMs default = 5 minutes
- priority default = 100
- Channel regex ^[CD][A-Z0-9]+$ boundaries (one-char tolerated today, flag if we tighten)
- Discriminated-union variance: auto_approve rejects reason/ttlMs, deny requires reason, require_approval accepts ttlMs default
- parsePolicyRules does NOT enforce duplicate id (documents deferred behavior for loader in 29-A.5)

Acceptance: 8+ passing assertions, imports from policy.ts, all paths in parsePolicyRule/parsePolicyRules exercised.
title: 29-A.1-followup: Test coverage for PolicyRule Zod schema

description: Block Kit threaded replies with correlation IDs. Per-channel audit: off|compact|full. Self-echo regression lock-in for allowBotIds. Rate-limit batching. Graceful failure — Slack API errors never block tool exec. Issue #30, ships v0.5.1 (not SOC2 audit — reframed as operator visibility). Blocked by 30-A, 32-B, 29-B.

description: New optional field on per-channel policy. Default 'off' (no projection). 'compact' = tool name + outcome. 'full' = redacted inputs + outcome.

description: Full flow: policy require:1 → Block Kit posts → approve → tool executes → receipt edited. Correlation ID in both Block Kit context and receipt context. Assert match.

description: Formal reference. Enumerate off/compact/full semantics. Warn about PII in full mode. Link 000-docs/audit-journal-architecture.md for the local-log counterpart.

description: Explain: 30-B Slack projection is operator visibility, 30-A local log is authoritative. Slack messages may be rate-limited or lost; local log is hash-chained and tamper-evident.

description: audit field on ChannelPolicy with values off|compact|full (dhh.1).

description: Receipt threading, correlation IDs, compact/full content projection (dhh.2-5).

description: Graceful Slack-API-down behavior and rate-limit batching (dhh.6-7).

description: Self-echo filter, audit:off silence, correlation-ID linking, and regression lock-in (dhh.8-10).

description: ACCESS.md audit-field docs + CLAUDE.md projection-vs-authoritative-log section (dhh.11-12).

description: Before tool execution, if audit!=off, post a stub threaded reply with :receipt: emoji + correlation ID in Block Kit context block. Store its ts for edit.

description: After tool execution, chat.update the receipt with exit code, path, summary. Edit (not new message) to keep thread clean. Handle rate-limit on update path.

description: Renders: 'tool: <name>  →  <outcome>'. Used when inputs could contain user context that shouldn't be echoed back into the channel.

description: Pulls redaction module from 30-A. Renders: 'tool: <name>\ninputs: <redacted>\noutcome: <x>'. Full mode requires opt-in because of the higher info density.

description: If chat.postMessage / chat.update fails during projection, log and continue. NEVER block tool execution. This is projection — the authoritative log is 30-A's local file.
title: 30-B.6: Graceful failure — Slack API error on journal post is log-only

description: Token bucket per channel. If burst exceeds 5/s, collapse to a single summary message linking to the local log path. Prevents Slack rate-limit outages from audit traffic.
title: 30-B.7: Rate-limit batching — >5 events/sec collapses to summary

description: Two-bot channel with allowBotIds. Bot A's projection messages must not trigger Bot A's gate() inbound. Locks in the fix from PR #33.

description:

Depends on the hash-chain writer (30-A earlier beads) landing first.

Acceptance:
- CLI: bun server.ts --verify-audit-log ~/.claude/channels/slack/audit.log
- Exit 0 on intact chain, non-zero with first-tampered-line number on mismatch
- Works on partial journals (truncated tail is not tamper)
- Documented in audit-journal-architecture.md

description: 3-4 concrete policy configs with expected decisions. Shows the first-applicable walk. Cross-link from ACCESS.md.

description: Formal schema reference (Zod → doc). Field-by-field. Note monotonicity constraint. Placeholder until 29-B lands runtime wiring.
title: 29-A.9: Docs — ACCESS.md policy-schema section

description: SessionSupervisor actor (Armstrong-style): activate, quiesce, deactivate. Widen outbound gate from channel to (channel, thread_ts). Widen permission-pairing key. Idle reaper (default 4h). slack/list_sessions MCP tool. Route peer-bot messages via supervisor. Issue #32, ships v0.5.0. Blocked by 32-A. Blocks 29-B, 30-B.
title: Supervise session lifecycle and recycle idle ones

description: Write interface comment block naming activate/quiesce/deactivate contract. Reference Joe Armstrong's supervision tree. Lives in lib.ts or a new supervisor.ts if the module grows large.
title: 32-B.1: Design doc — SessionSupervisor interface (Armstrong 2003)

description: Post-#33 peer-bot messages must land in the correct session. Update gate() delivery path to dispatch via supervisor.activate(key) + submit. Respects allowBotIds per-channel opt-in.
title: 32-B.10: Route peer-bot messages into thread's session via supervisor

description: Start session, begin tool call, advance clock past idle threshold. Reaper tick must NOT deactivate while in-flight map has entries. Deactivation happens only after tool call completes.
title: 32-B.11: Test — reaper respects in-flight tool calls

description: Describe the supervisor lifecycle in ARCHITECTURE.md. Reference session-state-machine.md for the diagrams. Note the Armstrong-style discipline.
title: 32-B.12: Docs — reaper + supervisor section in ARCHITECTURE.md

description: Supervisor actor interface (xa3.1), activate (xa3.2), quiesce (xa3.3).
title: Actor contract, activation, and quiesce

description: deactivate + persist (xa3.4), idle-TTL reaper w/ 4h default (xa3.8).
title: Deactivate sessions and reap idle ones

description: Thread-scoped tool calls (xa3.5), outbound-gate widening to (channel, thread_ts) (xa3.6), permission-pairing key widening (xa3.7).
title: Scope tool permissions to the current thread

description: Route peer-bot messages via supervisor (xa3.10), slack/list_sessions MCP tool (xa3.9).
title: Route peer messages and expose a list-sessions tool

description: Reaper-with-in-flight test (xa3.11) + architecture doc updates (xa3.12).
title: Reaper test and supervisor architecture notes

description: On activate: load session from disk via loader, allocate in-flight Map<requestId, AbortController>. Emit structured 'session.activate' log line with channel, thread, ownerId.
title: 32-B.2: Impl activate(key) — read state, register in-flight tool calls map

description: quiesce: stop accepting new tool calls for this key, wait for in-flight map to drain, emit 'session.quiesce' log. Idempotent.
title: 32-B.3: Impl quiesce(key) — refuse new work, await in-flight

description: deactivate: call persist() via atomic writer, release handle, remove from active map. Emit 'session.deactivate' log. Guaranteed to run after quiesce.
title: 32-B.4: Impl deactivate(key) — persist, release, emit close

description: Start two sessions (same channel, different threads). Submit tool call in thread A. Assert that completion post lands ONLY in thread A. Current code would let it leak to thread B.
title: 32-B.5: Failing test — tool call from thread A cannot post to thread B

description: Update lib.ts:assertOutboundAllowed to match on tuple not just channel. Update callers in server.ts. Security-critical (outbound gate layer).
title: 32-B.6: Widen assertOutboundAllowed to (channel, thread_ts) tuple

description: Update permission-relay handler in server.ts (~L876 — verify current line). Key prevents a Block Kit approval from one thread satisfying a permission ask from another thread.
title: 32-B.7: Widen permission-pairing key from requestId to (thread_ts, requestId)

description: setInterval tick that finds sessions whose lastActiveAt exceeds threshold. Calls quiesce() then deactivate(). Configurable via env var. Skips sessions with in-flight work.
title: 32-B.8: Idle reaper tick — SLACK_SESSION_IDLE_MS, default 4h

description: Register new MCP tool returning list of active (channel, thread_ts, lastActiveAt). Bounded output. DOES NOT return session state bodies (security — state may carry secrets).
title: 32-B.9: MCP tool slack/list_sessions — introspection
